name: The First Room (Composition Exemplar)
description: THE FIRST ROOM of the living world — a room containing an inhabitant (a cell holding a workflow-mandate = its JOB) and a payer (who escrows the reward from a conserved pool). The inhabitant does its mandated job step-by-step (each a receipted turn), finishes, the escrow releases, it is paid (a conserving transfer). Then the headline: a try-to-cheat battery — skip a prerequisite, overspend the budget, reach outside its compartment, take an ungranted verb, release the escrow without approval — each REFUSED in-band by the real executor, rendered in-room with the receipt-why. WELDS three landed organs (the colonist-job mandate, the escrow economy, the room model) into ONE runnable, cargo-testable scenario through one shared EmbeddedExecutor. gpui-free.

composition.note: first-room owns no verified primitive of its own; it COMPOSES other apps' organs through ONE shared EmbeddedExecutor. It is NOT a four-axis app — it has no FactoryDescriptor, no service interface, and no DeosApp (those belong to the organs it welds). It ships the one modern-app axis that fits a composition: the renderer-independent deos-view CARD that SHOWS the composed room.

organs.colonist_job: starbridge-compartment-workflow-mandate (the DAG mandate: no-skip · clearance · spend-budget)
organs.gateway: starbridge-storage-gateway-mandate (DAVID'S DOOR — the entry physics; see scenario::davids_door)

composition.entrypoint: run_first_room() -> Transcript (the honest cycle + the try-to-cheat battery)

axes.4_deos_view_card: card::room_card_value/json (src/card.rs) — the composed room as a deos.ui.* view-tree (genuine actions + in-room refusals, tagged distinctly)
